title: Cabinet papers – Data and Statistics (Census) Amendment Bill – Approval for introduction | Stats NZ
content:
## Cabinet papers – Data and Statistics (Census) Amendment Bill – Approval for introduction
16 March 2026, 2:07pm
The following Cabinet paper, briefings, letters and minutes of decision outline key decisions regarding the introduction of the Data and Statistics (Census) Amendment Bill.
The following papers have been approved for proactive release by the Minister of Statistics, Hon Dr Shane Reti:
- MM2970 Proposed approach to amending the Data and Statistics Act 2022
- MM3003 Proposals for amending the Data and Statistics Act
- MM3020 Aide memoire: Meeting with the Minister of Justice
- MM3026 Next Steps on census legislation
- MM2992 Consulting on a draft Census Bill and Cabinet paper
- MM3057 Supporting Cabinet decisions on changes to boundary reviews
- MM3038 Political party engagement on census modernisation and boundary review proposals
- MM3068 Ministerial and agency consultation on the Census Bill and Cabinet paper
- MM3098 Cabinet paper: Approval to introduce the Census Bill package
- MM3116 Approval to introduce the Census Bill
- Cross party consultation letters
- Cabinet paper: Data and Statistics (Census) Amendment Bill: Approval for Introduction
- Data and Statistics (Census) Amendment Bill
- Departmental Disclosure Statement
- Cabinet Legislation Committee - Minute of Decision Data and Statistics (Census) Amendment Bill: Approval for Introduction (LEG-26-MIN-0016), 19 February 2026
- Cabinet Minute of Decision - Report of the Cabinet Legislation Committee: Period Ended 20 February 2026 (CAB-6-MIN-0053), 23 February 2026
Please note that some content has been withheld under sections 9(2)(f)(iv), 9(2)(g)(i) and 9(2)(h) of the Official Information Act 1982. Content out of scope has also been redacted.
